title: Patient-Level Prediction
description: Patient-Level Prediction (PLP) trains machine learning models to predict individual patient outcomes within a specified time-at-risk window. Models include LASSO logistic regression, random forests, and gradient boosting.
tips: Define the prediction window carefully — too short misses late outcomes; too long captures unrelated events.
Minimum outcome prevalence of 1% is recommended for reliable model training.
LASSO logistic regression is the recommended starting model — it's interpretable and fast.
Review the AUC, calibration plot, and feature importance before deploying any prediction model.
PLP requires the HADES R runtime — check System Health if execution stalls.
